question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

graph.answer:
The library is centered on `nlohmann::json` (`basic_json<>`) and the runtime core is the `parse`/`dump` pair.

**Runtime Flow**
- Public type alias: [include/nlohmann/json_fwd.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json_fwd.hpp#L62)
- Public parse entrypoint: [include/nlohmann/json.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4090)
- Public dump entrypoint: [include/nlohmann/json.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L1338)

**Parse Path**
- `json::parse(...)` builds an input adapter and hands it to `detail::parser`, then calls `parser::parse(true, result)`: [include/nlohmann/json.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4090)
- `detail::parser::parse(...)` is the DOM-building wrapper; it chooses the callback-aware DOM parser when a callback is present, otherwise the normal DOM parser, then enforces strict EOF and error handling: [include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp#L95)
- The lexer does tokenization and dispatches to `scan_string()`, `scan_number()`, `scan_literal()`, or structural tokens: [include/nlohmann/detail/input/lexer.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/lexer.hpp#L1610)
- The actual DOM mutation happens in `json_sax_dom_parser`, which handles `null`, numbers, strings, arrays, objects, keys, and parse errors: [include/nlohmann/detail/input/json_sax.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/json_sax.hpp#L167)

**Dump Path**
- `json::dump(...)` creates a `serializer` and delegates the actual work to it: [include/nlohmann/json.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L1338)
- `detail::serializer::dump(...)` recursively walks objects, arrays, primitives, and binary values: [include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L107)
- String escaping and UTF-8 error handling live in `dump_escaped(...)`: [include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L391)
- Stream output uses the same serializer path via `operator<<`: [include/nlohmann/json.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4050)

**Nearby Paths**
- `accept(...)` is the parse-only validation branch that reuses the same parser core: [include/nlohmann/json.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4133)
- `sax_parse(...)` is the event-driven branch, and binary formats are routed separately there: [include/nlohmann/json.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4163)
- If you touch binary formats next, the neighboring `from_cbor` / `to_cbor` / `from_msgpack` / `to_msgpack` families are in the same section of `json.hpp`: [include/nlohmann/json.hpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4348)

**Tests To Read Next**
- End-to-end parse/accept/SAX/stream behavior: [tests/src/unit-deserialization.cpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-deserialization.cpp#L237)
- Parser internals, callbacks, comments, trailing commas, and direct `json::parser(...)` usage: [tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp#L349)
- The parser helper and callback/relaxed-input flow start here: [tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp#L217)
- Lexer tokenization and invalid-token edge cases: [tests/src/unit-class_lexer.cpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-class_lexer.cpp#L35)
- Dump formatting, UTF-8 error handling, binary serialization, and floating-point formatting: [tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp#L18)
- UTF-8 and `error_handler_t` coverage for `dump(...)`: [tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp#L83)
- Direct `dump_escaped(...)` unit coverage: [tests/src/unit-convenience.cpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-convenience.cpp#L107)
- Parsing arbitrary containers and iterators: [tests/src/unit-user_defined_input.cpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-user_defined_input.cpp#L18)
- Unicode round-trips and surrogate handling: [tests/src/unit-unicode1.cpp](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-unicode1.cpp#L21)

If you want, I can turn this into a “first behavior change” reading order next and point you to the exact files for either input-side changes or output-side changes.
